_publications._publicationTitle: Modulation of Ras signaling alters the toxicity of hydroquinone, a benzene metabolite and component of cigarette smoke.

_contextSnippet: There was a higher recovery of Lin- BM cells from the Nf1−/− mice (0.7%) than the WT littermates (0.3%). The greater recovery of Lin- BM cells in the Nf1−/− mice is likely indicative of chronic polyclonal hyperproliferation
resourceName: Mx1-Cre, Nf1 flox/flox mice (129SV × C57BL/6 J)
details: Higher recovery of Lin- BM cells from Nf1−/− mice (0.7%) compared to WT littermates (0.3%), likely due to chronic polyclonal hyperproliferation from hypersensitivity to GM-CSF
